justification: All 7 expected rules identified (plus an extra BR-5 for manager authorization). Invariant classifications are correct: accrual rate (false), negative balance (true), 3-day advance (false), medical retroactive (false), rejected leave balance (true), contract end date (true), carryover cap (false). BR-5 manager authorization marked invariant:true is defensible. Multiple ambiguities surfaced including contract end-date inclusivity, partial month accrual, 5-day window calendar vs working days, and carry-over sequencing with pending leave. 12 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria far exceed the minimum 4. Confidence level of 72 is well-calibrated given the unspecified operational details.

observations.text: All 7 required business rules identified with correct invariant classification — no misclassifications
Accrual rate and carryover cap correctly marked invariant:false; negative balance, rejected-leave balance integrity, and contract boundary correctly marked invariant:true
12 acceptance criteria with clear Given/When/Then structure, covering accrual, negative balance, medical leave exception, carryover, advance notice, and contract boundary
Rich ambiguity surfacing: contract end-date inclusivity, partial month accrual, carry-over sequencing, 5-day window day type — exceeds the minimum requirement
Added BR-5 (manager authorization) as a bonus rule with sound invariant:true justification
Ubiquitous language section is thorough and well-defined
Edge cases section is exceptionally detailed with 8 scenarios covering subtle interactions
Did not explicitly distinguish labor-law-adjacent rules from HR policy rules as separate categories, though the invariant justifications implicitly make this distinction
